English coach, [[Jamie Day (footballer, born 1979)|Jamie Day]] led the team to elimination from the [[2018 SAFF Championship]] on home soil.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/news/nepal-ko-bangladesh-1631065|title=Bangladesh's sorry record continues|date=8 September 2018|website=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504141059/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/news/nepal-ko-bangladesh-1631065|url-status=live}}</ref> However, Bangladesh progressed to the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round]] of the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] by defeating [[Laos national football team|Laos]] 1–0 on aggregate.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/follow-bangladesh-v-laos-live-1755490 |title=Bangladesh through after goalless draw |work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|date=11 June 2019 |access-date=11 June 2019 |archive-date=13 June 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190613132341/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/follow-bangladesh-v-laos-live-1755490 |url-status=live}}</ref> Drawn into a group alongside India, [[Afghanistan national football team|Afghanistan]], [[Oman national football team|Oman]], and [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]], who had already qualified for the World Cup as host, their only point in the first phase came from a 1–1 draw against India at the [[Salt Lake Stadium]] in [[Kolkata]].<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.tbsnews.net/sports/topu-barmans-late-goal-helps-bangladesh-draw-1-1-against-afghanistan-255550|title=Topu Barman's late goal helps Bangladesh draw 1-1 against Afghanistan|date=4 June 2021|work=[[The Business Standard]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504141800/https://www.tbsnews.net/sports/topu-barmans-late-goal-helps-bangladesh-draw-1-1-against-afghanistan-255550|url-status=live}}</ref> The second phase resumed in December 2020 in a centralised hub in Qatar due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]]. Bangladesh collected one point from their remaining four matches, drawing 1–1 with Afghanistan. Despite finishing bottom of Group E, they advanced to the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification|2023 AFC Asian Cup qualifiers]] as one of the best fifth-placed teams.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-final-round-asian-cup-qualifiers-2112061 |title=Bangladesh into final round of Asian Cup Qualifiers|first=Atique|last=Anam|date=16 June 2021|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=13 January 2022 |archive-date=13 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220113124236/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-final-round-asian-cup-qualifiers-2112061 |url-status=live}}</ref>

In March 2021, Bangladesh finished as runners-up in the [[2021 Three Nations Cup (Nepal)]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://kathmandupost.com/football/2021/03/29/nepal-defeat-bangladesh-2-1-to-clinch-the-three-nations-cup|title=Nepal defeat Bangladesh 2-1 to clinch Three Nations Cup|work=[[The Kathmandu Post]]|date=29 March 2021|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504142554/https://kathmandupost.com/football/2021/03/29/nepal-defeat-bangladesh-2-1-to-clinch-the-three-nations-cup|url-status=live}}</ref>  In September 2021, following a series of poor results, Day was placed on temporary leave ahead of the [[2021 SAFF Championship]], where the team was eliminated in the first round under interim coach [[Óscar Bruzón]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://bdnews24.com/sport/late-penalty-heartbreak-for-10-man-bangladesh-as-nepal-reach-saff-championship-final |title=Late penalty heartbreak for 10-man Bangladesh as Nepal reach SAFF Championship final |work=[[bdnews24.com]]|date=13 October 2021 |access-date=13 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211120075913/https://bdnews24.com/sport/2021/10/13/late-penalty-heartbreak-for-10-man-bangladesh-as-nepal-reach-saff-championship-final|archive-date=20 November 2021|url-status=live}}</ref> 

[[File:Topu Barman in action against Maldives in the 2021 SAFF Championship.jpg|thumb|left|Bangladesh against Maldives at the [[2021 SAFF Championship]].]]

Under Spanish coach [[Javier Fernández Cabrera|Javier Cabrera]], the team was eliminated from the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round]] after suffering defeats in all three games.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.newagebd.net/article/173294/bangladesh-suffer-malaysia-mauling|title=Bangladesh suffer Malaysia mauling|work=[[New Age (Bangladesh)|New Age]]|date=14 June 2022|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504153928/https://www.newagebd.net/article/173294/bangladesh-suffer-malaysia-mauling|archive-date=4 May 2026|url-status=live}}</ref>  At the [[2023 SAFF Championship]] in [[Bengaluru]], India, Bangladesh reached the semi-finals for the first time in six editions, but were eliminated by [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]] after conceding in extra time.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/brilliant-bangladesh-go-down-fighting-3358986 |title=Brilliant Bangladesh go down fighting|date=1 July 2023|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504154707/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/brilliant-bangladesh-go-down-fighting-3358986|url-status=live}}</ref> In the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round]], Bangladesh defeated Maldives 2–1 on aggregate.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-clinch-wc-qualifiers-spot-3446156|title=Bangladesh clinch WC Qualifiers spot|date=18 October 2023|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504155104/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-clinch-wc-qualifiers-spot-3446156|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round]], Bangladesh were drawn into a group with Australia, Lebanon and Palestine, losing all of their matches<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-taste-4-0-loss-lebanons-matouk-marks-farewell-hattrick-3632666|title=Bangladesh taste 4-0 loss against Lebanon to end 2026 WC qualifiers campaign|date=11 June 2024|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504155717/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-taste-4-0-loss-lebanons-matouk-marks-farewell-hattrick-3632666|url-status=live}}</ref> except for a 1–1 home draw against Lebanon.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21|title=Morsalin screamer earns Bangladesh a point|url=https://en.prothomalo.com/sports/football/juwx58ufd5|access-date=2023-11-21|website=[[Prothom Alo|Prothom Alo English]]|language=en|archive-date=18 November 2025|archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/k5ZSa|url-status=live}}</ref> Despite limited improvement in the team's standing ahead of the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round]], the BFF extended Cabrera's contract until 2026, making him the longest-serving head coach in the country's history.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/cabrera-set-be-longest-serving-bangladesh-coach-3801066 |title=Cabrera set to be longest-serving Bangladesh coach |date=17 January 2025 |work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=7 March 2025 |archive-date=7 March 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250307085545/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/cabrera-set-be-longest-serving-bangladesh-coach-3801066 |url-status=live }}</ref> During the qualification campaign, Bangladesh introduced several [[Bangladeshi diaspora|diaspora]] players, including [[Shamit Shome]], [[Zayyan Ahmed]], [[Fahamedul Islam]] and [[Leicester City]] midfielder [[Hamza Choudhury]]. However, the team failed to qualify after finishing third in their group with two draws and three defeats, despite recording a notable victory 1–0 over regional rivals India, their first in 22 years.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/morsalin-goal-seals-1-0-win-bangladesh-beat-india-after-22-years-4038026|title=Morsalin goal seals 1-0 win as Bangladesh beat India after 22 years|date=18 November 2025|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=4 May 2026|archive-date=4 May 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260504155717/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bangladesh-taste-4-0-loss-lebanons-matouk-marks-farewell-hattrick-3632666|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Team image==
===Kit===
{{multiple image
| align             = right
| direction         = vertical
| image1            = Bangladesh at the 1975 Merdeka Tournament.png
| width1            = 
| image2            = Bangladesh national football team in the 1998 FIFA World Cup qualifiers.png
| footer            = Transition of home kits from [[1975 Merdeka Tournament|1975 Merdeka Cup]] (top), [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|1998 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] (middle) and [[2021 SAFF Championship]] (bottom).
| image3            = Bangladesh National Football Team in Maldives in the SAFF Championship 2021.jpg
| total_width       = 
| width2            = 
| caption1          = 
| caption2          = 
| width3            =
| caption3          =
}}

{{Commons|Bangladesh national football team kits}}

The national team has traditionally alternated between green-red, white, and orange home jerseys, while the away or secondary kit has varied between red, white, and orange. Prior to the turn of the century, the team featured the [[Bangladesh flag|national flag of Bangladesh]] (dark green background with red circle) on their jerseys, after which they switched to using the [[Bangladesh Football Federation|BFF]] logo.

In the team’s inaugural year, Bangladesh participated in the [[1973 Merdeka Tournament|1973 Merdeka Cup]] in Malaysia, wearing white shirts and shorts with black socks. During the [[1980 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] Group 2, held in [[Dhaka]], Bangladesh played their first official home matches, sporting maroon shirts paired with white shorts and matching socks. In the [[1980 AFC Asian Cup|main round]], the team wore orange shirts with white shorts and orange socks, while also using a secondary kit consisting of red shirts with white shorts and socks. 

Between 1980 and 1999, the team's primary kit alternated between orange, green, and white shirts. Following the turn of the century, Bangladesh predominantly adopted a traditional red and green home kit, although during their [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup]] triumph, they notably wore blue and white shirts with red shoulder stripes, complemented by white shorts and socks.

Bangladesh wore kits manufactured by [[Adidas]] during the periods 1998, 2005–2007, and 2013–2014. During their [[Football at the 1999 South Asian Games|1999 South Asian Games]] gold medal triumph, the team wore green [[FBT (company)|FBT]] shirts with white shoulder stripes, green shorts, and green socks. During their tour of [[England]] in 2000, the team's jerseys were produced by Sapphire Sports. 

In April 2009, [[Nokia]], in its second year as the national team's sponsor, became the team's jersey sponsor as part of the renewed agreement.<ref name="Nokia2">{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/news-detail-85325|title=Nokia renews BFF sponsorship|date=24 April 2009|work=The Daily Star |access-date=8 February 2025|archive-date=8 February 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250208144857/https://www.thedailystar.net/news-detail-85325|url-status=live}}</ref> As the team had been using the same kit for nearly five years, the federation organized a jersey design contest ahead of the [[2021 SAFF Championship]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.daily-bangladesh.com/english/sports/55678|title=BFF creates a chance to be a proud national team jersey designer|work=Daily Bangladesh|access-date=18 September 2022|archive-date=20 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220920171520/https://www.daily-bangladesh.com/english/sports/55678|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Grand Sport Group]] manufactured the team's kits for the [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup]]. 

In 2015, the team used green home kits and red away kits produced by FBT for the [[2015 Bangabandhu Cup]] and the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round]]. For the [[2016 Bangabandhu Cup]], Bangladesh wore kits made by [[Lotto Sport Italia]].

On 11 February 2025, after a decade without a kit sponsor, the BFF signed a two-year deal with the local sportswear manufacturer Dour. This agreement saw Bangladesh reintroduce white shirts incorporating traditional [[Jamdani]] motifs, along with white shorts and socks.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bff-rope-dour-national-teams-kit-sponsor-3821736|title=BFF ropes in Dour as national teams' kit sponsor|work=The Daily Star|date=11 February 2025|access-date=16 March 2025|archive-date=16 March 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250316074630/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bff-rope-dour-national-teams-kit-sponsor-3821736|url-status=live}}</ref> Additionally, the English typography of "Bangladesh" was stylized into the shape of the country's map and displayed on the back of both the home and away jerseys.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.prothomalo.com/lifestyle/fw5l758lg6|script-title=bn:হামজা-জামালদের নতুন হোম জার্সিতে জামদানির মোটিফ ছাড়াও আরও যা যা আছে|trans-title=Hamza-Jamal's new home jersey features more than just Jamdani's motif|work=[[Prothom Alo]]|language=bn|date=17 May 2025|access-date=3 June 2026|archive-date=3 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260603151650/https://www.prothomalo.com/lifestyle/fw5l758lg6|url-status=live}}</ref>

====Kit suppliers====
{|class="wikitable"
! style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color: #FFFFFF; text-align:center;"|Period
! style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color: #FFFFFF; text-align:center;"|Kit Manufacturer
! style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color: #FFFFFF; "| {{Abbr|Ref|References}}
|-
| 1973–1998
|  colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"|N/A

|-
| 1998
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.ha7classicalshirts.co.uk/products/bangladesh-1998-00-matchworn-shirt-7-adidas-size-medium |title=Bangladesh 1998/00 Matchworn Shirt #7 - Adidas - Size Medium |website=HA7 Classical Shirts |access-date=1 November 2022 |archive-date=1 November 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221101175445/https://www.ha7classicalshirts.co.uk/products/bangladesh-1998-00-matchworn-shirt-7-adidas-size-medium |url-status=live}}</ref>
|-
| 1999
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|Thailand}} [[FBT (company)|FBT]]

|-

|<ref>{{cite web |title=Bangladesh (Home 2001 - Sapphire Sports) |url=https://www.theglobalobsession.com/2010/10/bangladesh.html |website=The Global Obsession |access-date=14 November 2023}}</ref><ref name="kits98topresent">{{cite web |title=Bangladesh Kit History |url=https://www.footballkitarchive.com/bangladesh-kits-t1527/ |website=Football Kit Archive |access-date=14 November 2023}}</ref>
|-
| 2001–2004
| style="text-align:left;"|None
|
|-
| 2005–2007
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|
|-
| 2008–2009
| style="text-align:left;"|None
|-
| 2010
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|Thailand}} [[Grand Sport Group|Grand Sport]]
|<ref name="kits98topresent"/>
|-
| 2011–2012
| style="text-align:left;"|None
|
|-
| 2013–2014
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|<ref name="kits98topresent"/>
|-
| 2015
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|Thailand}} [[FBT (company)|FBT]]
|<ref name="kits98topresent"/>
|-
| 2016
|style="text-align:left" |{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]]
|<ref>{{cite news |title=Sponsors come forward to make Bangabandhu Gold Cup a success |url=https://thedailynewnation.com/news/79544/Sponsors-come-forward-to-make-Bangabandhu-Gold-Cup-a-success |access-date=14 November 2023 |work=The New Nation |date=8 January 2016 |language=en |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231114033352/https://thedailynewnation.com/news/79544/Sponsors-come-forward-to-make-Bangabandhu-Gold-Cup-a-success |archive-date=14 November 2023}}</ref>
|-
| 2016–2025
| style="text-align:left;"|None
| <ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.prothomalo.com/sports/football/%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%85%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A1%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A1%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B8%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%AA%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%9A%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%82%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6-%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%9F%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%B2-%E0%A6%A6%E0%A6%B2|script-title=bn:ফ্রিতে অ্যাডিডাসের প্রচারে বাংলাদেশ ফুটবল দল?|trans-title=Bangladesh football team in Adidas promotion for free?|date=21 March 2018|work=[[Prothom Alo]]|language=bn|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=21 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220921185430/https://www.prothomalo.com/sports/football/%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%85%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A1%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A1%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B8%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%AA%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%9A%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%82%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6-%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%9F%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%B2-%E0%A6%A6%E0%A6%B2|url-status=live}}</ref>
|-
| 2025–present
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|BAN}} Dour 
|<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.channel24bd.tv/sports/article/252235/%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%9B%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%9A%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%95%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%82%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6-%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%9F%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%B2-%E0%A6%A6%E0%A6%B2%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%95%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%9F-%E0%A6%B8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AA%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%B0-%E2%80%98%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%8C%E0%A7%9C%E2%80%99|script-title=bn:দুই বছরের চুক্তিতে বাংলাদেশ ফুটবল দলের কিট স্পন্সর 'দৌড়'|trans-title='Dour' to sponsor Bangladesh football team's kit in two-year deal|work=channel24bd.tv|language=bn|date=8 February 2025|access-date=8 February 2025|archive-date=8 February 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250208143957/https://www.channel24bd.tv/sports/article/252235/%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%9B%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%9A%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%95%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%82%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6-%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%9F%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%B2-%E0%A6%A6%E0%A6%B2%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%95%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%9F-%E0%A6%B8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AA%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%B0-%E2%80%98%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%8C%E0%A7%9C%E2%80%99|url-status=live}}</ref>
|-
|}

===Sponsors===
The national team was sponsored by [[Nokia]] for a two-year period from 2008 to 2010.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/news-detail-26002|title=Nokia connects football|date=4 March 2008|work=The Daily Star |access-date=12 January 2024|archive-date=12 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112182929/https://www.thedailystar.net/news-detail-26002|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Nokia2"/> On 8 June 2019, India's [[TVS Motor Company]] announced its two-year sponsorship tie-up with BFF, sponsoring the national team during the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/industry/services/advertising/tvs-motor-becomes-official-sponsor-of-bangladesh-football-team/articleshow/69702418.cms?from=mdr|title=TVS Motor becomes official sponsor of Bangladesh football team|work=The Economic Times|date=8 June 2019|access-date=16 September 2022|archive-date=20 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220920163418/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/industry/services/advertising/tvs-motor-becomes-official-sponsor-of-bangladesh-football-team/articleshow/69702418.cms?from=mdr|url-status=live}}</ref> On 15 March 2026, [[United Commercial Bank PLC|United Commercial Bank]] signed a five-year deal to sponsor the men's senior national team.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/hamza-onboard-bff-gets-mens-team-sponsor-3848781|title=With Hamza onboard, BFF gets men's team sponsor|work=The Daily Star|date=15 March 2025|access-date=16 March 2025|archive-date=16 March 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250316074406/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/hamza-onboard-bff-gets-mens-team-sponsor-3848781|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Bangladesh B===
The [[Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|President's Gold Cup]] was an annual football tournament held in [[Dhaka]], Bangladesh, primarily from 1981 to 1993. During this period, the [[Bangladesh Football Federation|BFF]] often entered two teams: Bangladesh Red and Green (1981–1986; 1993) and Bangladesh White and Blue (1987). Secondary teams were generally composed of youth or reserve players. Many participating teams used the tournament as a platform for developmental or non-senior squads. Fielding multiple national sides was common among South Asian countries during the 1980s and 1990s, with Nepal ([[Nepal national football B team|Nepal B]]) and Pakistan ([[Pakistan national football B team|Pakistan B]]) also operating second-string team's.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://onnodristy.com/%e0%a7%a9%e0%a7%9f-%e0%a6%aa%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%b0%e0%a7%87%e0%a6%b8%e0%a6%bf%e0%a6%a1%e0%a7%87%e0%a6%a8%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%9f-%e0%a6%97%e0%a7%8b%e0%a6%b2%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%a1%e0%a6%95%e0%a6%be%e0%a6%aa/ |script-title=bn:৩য় প্রেসিডেন্ট গোল্ডকাপ ফুটবল প্রতিযোগিতা ১৯৮৩|trans-title=3rd President's Gold Cup Football Competition 1983 |date=15 May 2020 |language=bn |website=onnodristy.com|access-date=24 March 2022 |archive-date=14 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220214142617/https://onnodristy.com/%e0%a7%a9%e0%a7%9f-%e0%a6%aa%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%b0%e0%a7%87%e0%a6%b8%e0%a6%bf%e0%a6%a1%e0%a7%87%e0%a6%a8%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%9f-%e0%a6%97%e0%a7%8b%e0%a6%b2%e0%a7%8d%e0%a6%a1%e0%a6%95%e0%a6%be%e0%a6%aa/ |url-status=live}}</ref> 

In the [[1989 Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|1989 edition]], Bangladesh Red, consisting of senior international players, won the tournament by defeating the [[Korea University]] football team on penalties, after eliminating Bangladesh Green (junior team) in the semi-finals.<ref name="GoldCup989"/> The victory is not recognized as Bangladesh's first international trophy, as the competition primarily featured club sides.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.bd-pratidin.com/sport-news/2020/01/15/492307 |script-title=bn:ফুটবলে চ্যাম্পিয়ন ৫ অধিনায়ক|trans-title=5 captains who are champions in football |date=15 January 2020 |work=Bangladesh Pratidin |language=bn |access-date=24 March 2022 |archive-date=11 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220611130300/https://www.bd-pratidin.com/sport-news/2020/01/15/492307 |url-status=live}}</ref> In the [[1987 Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|1987 edition]], Bangladesh White and Blue participated, with the White team mainly drawn from [[Mohammedan SC (Dhaka)|Mohammedan SC]] and the Blue team from [[Abahani Limited Dhaka|Dhaka Abahani]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://khela71.com/who-is-the-top-goal-scorer-for-bangladesh/ |script-title=bn:বাংলাদেশের 'সর্বোচ্চ গোলদাতা' বিষয়ক বিতর্ক|trans-title=The controversy over Bangladesh's 'highest goalscorer' |language=bn |access-date=24 March 2022 |website=khela71.com |archive-date=22 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220122105146/https://khela71.com/who-is-the-top-goal-scorer-for-bangladesh/ |url-status=live |date=27 June 2020}}</ref> However, the [[World Football Elo Ratings]] recognize the White team as the senior national team.

===Home stadium===
{{main|List of football stadiums in Bangladesh}}
[[File:Bangabandhu National Stadium, Dhaka, Bangladesh.jpg|right|thumb|[[National Stadium, Dhaka]]]]

The Bangladesh team plays the majority of its home matches at the [[National Stadium, Dhaka|National Stadium]] in [[Dhaka]]. Built in 1954, it was one of the venues used by the [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan national team]] for home matches prior to the [[Independence of Bangladesh]]. Bangladesh played its first home match at the stadium against the [[Afghanistan national football team|Afghanistan]] in a [[1980 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] match, which ended in a 2–2 draw, with [[Mohammad Abdul Halim]] scoring both goals for Bangladesh.<ref>{{cite news|title=Bangladesh hold Afghanistan 2–2|publisher=[[The Bangladesh Times]]|page=1|date=2 March 1979|language=en}}</ref>

In 1988, the [[Mirpur Stadium]] was constructed exclusively for football and hosted the [[1989 Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|1989]] and [[1993 Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|1993]] editions of the [[Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|President's Gold Cup]]. However, following a prolonged dispute between the [[Bangladesh Football Federation|BFF]] and the [[Bangladesh Cricket Board|BCB]], the National Stadium was allocated solely to the BFF, while the BCB assumed control of the Mirpur Stadium at the end of the 2004–05 season.<ref>{{cite web |last=Miller |first=Andrew |date=2005 |title=Bangabandhu National Stadium |url=http://www.espncricinfo.com/bangladesh/content/ground/56661.html |access-date=20 May 2024 |website=[[ESPNcricinfo]]}}</ref>

The National Stadium has hosted the [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup|2003]], [[2009 SAFF Championship|2009]], and [[2018 SAFF Championship|2018]] editions of the [[SAFF Championship]], and was also one of the venues for the [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite news |last= |first= |date=29 May 2020 |title=AFC lists Bangabandhu Stadium as 'iconic' |url=https://www.daily-sun.com/printversion/details/484295 |access-date=20 May 2024 |work=[[Daily Sun (Bangladesh)|Daily Sun]] |language=en|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605141709/https://www.daily-sun.com/printversion/details/484295|url-status=live}}</ref> Prior to renovations in 2011, the stadium had a capacity of nearly 55,000. This was reduced to 36,000 after redevelopment, and further decreased to 22,400 following renovations carried out between 2021 and 2025.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/top-news/news/bangabandhu-national-stadium-expensive-facelift-3408671|title=Bangabandhu National Stadium: An expensive facelift|date=2 September 2023|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|language=en|access-date=5 June 2026 |archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605142156/https://www.thedailystar.net/top-news/news/bangabandhu-national-stadium-expensive-facelift-3408671|url-status=live}}</ref>

Occasionally, home matches are also held at the [[Sheikh Kamal Stadium, Nilphamari|Sheikh Kamal Stadium]] in [[Nilphamari District|Nilphamari]], the [[Sylhet District Stadium]] in [[Sylhet]], the [[Rajshahi District Stadium]] in [[Rajshahi]], the [[Cox's Bazar Stadium]] in [[Cox's Bazar]], the [[Shamsul Huda Stadium]] in [[Jessore]], and the [[Bashundhara Kings Arena]] in Dhaka.

===Supporters===
{{multiple image
| align             = right
| direction         = vertical
| image1            = Bangladeshi Football Ultras (2023) Bangladesh vs Lebanon.jpg
| width1            = 
| image2            = Bangladeshi Football Ultras - Bangladesh vs Palestine.jpg
| footer            = Bangladesh football fans at the [[Bashundhara Kings Arena]] during the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match against [[Lebanon national football team|Lebanon]] (above) and [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] (below)
| total_width       = 
| width2            = 
| caption1          = 
| caption2          = 
}}

The [[Dhaka derby]] between [[Mohammedan SC (Dhaka)|Mohammedan]] and [[Abahani Limited Dhaka|Dhaka Abahani]] has historically drawn higher attendances than many national team fixtures. However, the [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup]] held at the [[National Stadium, Dhaka]] recorded large crowds, including approximately 55,000 spectators for the match against [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] and around 46,000 for the final against [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffgold02.html|title=SAFF Championship 2003|access-date=16 April 2023|website=[[RSSSF]]|archive-date=24 March 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240324140707/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffgold02.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

Football matches in other parts of the country have also attracted significant attendances relative to stadium capacity. In 2014, a friendly between the [[Bangladesh national under-23 football team|Bangladesh U23]] and [[Nepal national under-23 football team|Nepal U23]] at the [[Sylhet District Stadium]] drew an estimated 50,000 spectators, exceeding typical capacity levels. More than 10,000 spectators reportedly forced entry through the stadium gate and entered the field after finding no available space in the 25,000 capacity gallery, initially leading both teams to refuse to play. Eventually, spectators were accommodated along the sidelines under police supervision. In the same year, the [[Shamsul Huda Stadium]] in Jessore, which has a listed capacity of 12,000, hosted more than 30,000 spectators during a friendly against [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.risingbd.com/english/editorial/news/19017|title=Bangladesh Football sees hope of golden days|work=Risingbd|access-date=24 June 2022|archive-date=21 April 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220421021155/https://www.risingbd.com/english/editorial/news/19017|url-status=live}}</ref> 

Interest in national team fixtures increased in subsequent years, particularly following the inclusion of diaspora players such as [[Hamza Choudhury]] and [[Shamit Shome]], with strong demand for tickets reported for competitive matches. During a [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round]] home fixture against [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]], approximately 19,000 tickets were sold out within 24 minutes of release.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/19000-tickets-snapped-24-minutes-bangladesh-hong-kong-clash-3996671|title=19,000 tickets snapped up in 24 minutes for Bangladesh–Hong Kong clash|date=28 September 2025|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|language=en |access-date=5 June 2026|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605143655/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/19000-tickets-snapped-24-minutes-bangladesh-hong-kong-clash-3996671|url-status=live}}</ref> In the same qualification cycle, tickets for the home game against [[India national football team|India]] were reported to have sold out rapidly, with general seating allocations being exhausted within about 6 minutes of going on sale.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://en.prothomalo.com/sports/football/glh72svlny|title=Bangladesh-India match: Gallery tickets sold out within 6 minutes|date=10 November 2025|work=[[Prothom Alo]]|language=en |access-date=5 June 2026|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605144232/https://en.prothomalo.com/sports/football/glh72svlny|url-status=live}}</ref> This heightened demand led to a November 2025 proposal by the [[National Sports Council]] to construct a dedicated football stadium in [[Purbachal]], aimed at reducing reliance on the National Stadium, which, following renovations (2021–2025), has a reduced capacity of 22,400.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bff-seeks-purbachal-land-dedicated-stadium-4170121|title=dedicated |date=7 May 2026|work=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|language=en |access-date=5 June 2026|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605144426/https://www.thedailystar.net/sports/football/news/bff-seeks-purbachal-land-dedicated-stadium-4170121|url-status=live}}</ref>

Organised supporter activity includes the Bangladeshi Football Ultras, established in 2019, which supports the men’s, [[Bangladesh women's national football team|women’s]], and youth national teams.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-03-28 |title=BD Ultras: Die-hard fans of Bangladesh football |url=https://en.prothomalo.com/video/nk5xgxplmx|access-date=2025-08-17|website=Prothomalo|language=en|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605143431/https://en.prothomalo.com/video/nk5xgxplmx|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Bangladesh national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

| time      = 20:00 [[Bangladesh Standard Time|BST]] 
| round     = [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2027 ACQ – 3R]]
| score     = 1–0
| report    = [https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/afc_asian_cup.html/news/qualifiers-group-c-bangladesh-1-0-india Report]
| team1     = {{fb-rt|BAN}}
| goals1    =
*[[Shekh Morsalin|Morsalin]] {{goal|11}}
| team2     = {{fb|IND}}
| goals2    =
| stadium   = [[National Stadium, Dhaka|National Stadium]]
| location  = {{nobr|[[Dhaka]], [[Bangladesh]]}}
| attendance= 23,712
| referee   = Clifford Daypuyat ([[Philippine Football Federation|Philippines]])
| result    = W
}}

==Players==
{{See also|List of Bangladesh international footballers}}

===Current squad===
The following 23 players were named in the squad for the [[Exhibition game#International|FIFA Tier-1 Friendly]] against [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]] on 3 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web |title=23 names. One goal. 🇧🇩 Bangladesh's 23-player squad list for the FIFA Tier-1 Friendly against San Marino is here! |url=https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1Coc77sBUX/ |website=[[Facebook]] |publisher=[[Bangladesh Football Federation]] |date=30 May 2026 |access-date=30 May 2026 |language=en}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 5 June 2026 after the game against [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=GK|name=[[Mitul Marma]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2003|12|11}}|caps=21 |goals=0 |club=[[Dhaka Abahani]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Sujon Hossain]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1996|8|5}}|caps=1 |goals=0 |club=[[Mohammedan SC (Dhaka)|Mohammedan]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Anisur Rahman Zico]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1997|8|10}}|caps=32 |goals=0 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Tariq Kazi]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|10|6|df=y}}|caps=38 |goals=2 |club=[[Paro FC]]|clubnat=BHU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Topu Barman]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|20|df=y}}|caps=70|goals=8 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}

{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[Bishwanath Ghosh (footballer)|Bishwanath Ghosh]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|5|30|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=0 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=DF|name=[[Md Saad Uddin]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|9|1|df=y}}|caps=49 |goals=2 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=DF|name=[[Md Taj Uddin]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|1|5|df=y}}|caps=4 |goals=0 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Isa Faysal]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|20|df=y}}|caps=16 |goals=0 |club=[[Bangladesh Police FC|Bangladesh Police]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Jamal Bhuyan]]|other=[[captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1990|4|10}}|caps=94 |goals=1 |club=[[Brothers Union]]|clubnat=BAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=MF|name=[[Sohel Rana (footballer, born 1995)|Sohel Rana]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1995|3|27}}|caps=81 |goals=1 |club=[[Bashundhara Kings]]|clubnat=BAN}}

{{nat fs g end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Bangladesh squad within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mohammed Al-Amin|Al-Amin]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2004|3|29}}|caps=2 |goals=0 |club=[[Dhaka Abahani]]|clubnat=BAN|latest=v. {{fb|NEP}}; 13 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mohammad Ibrahim (footballer, born 1997)|Mohammad Ibrahim]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1997|8|7}}|caps=42 |goals=4 |club=[[Dhaka Abahani]]|clubnat=BAN|latest=v. {{fb|SMR}}; 5 June 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Arif Hossain (footballer)|Arif Hossain]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2001|12|31}}|caps=1 |goals=0 |club=[[Mohammedan SC (Dhaka)|Mohammedan]]|clubnat=BAN|latest=v. {{fb|NEP}}; 6 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>INJ</sup> Withdrew due to injury<br />
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad / standby<br />
<sup>SUS</sup> Serving suspension<br />
<sup>WD</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|5 June 2026.}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Zimmerman |first1=Benjamin |title=Bangladesh |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/17/Bangladesh.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=27 May 2022 |archive-date=2 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230202061121/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/17/2023/Bangladesh.html |url-status=live}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Bangladesh.''

:<small>'''NB''' The list is inaccurate as caps for long serving players such as [[Ashish Bhadra]], [[Satyajit Das Rupu]], [[Badal Roy (footballer)|Badal Roy]] & [[Khurshid Alam Babul]], along with many others - are still unknown by the [[Bangladesh Football Federation]] and is still being researched.</small><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.prothomalo.com/sports/football/%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%82%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%B9%E0%A7%9F%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%9A%E0%A7%87%E0%A7%9F%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%AC%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B6%E0%A6%BF-%E0%A6%AE%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9A-%E0%A6%95%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B0|date=13 June 2022|access-date=29 May 2025|script-title=bn:বাংলাদেশের হয়ে সবচেয়ে বেশি ম্যাচ কার|first=Masud|last=Alam|work=Prothom Alo|language=bn}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.prothomalo.com/sports/%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%95%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9B%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%87-%E0%A6%86%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%9C%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%95-%E0%A6%AE%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9A%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%B9%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AC-|script-title=bn:বাফুফের কাছে নেই আন্তর্জাতিক ম্যাচের হিসাব!|work=Prothom Alo|language=bn|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=2 January 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230102154740/https://www.prothomalo.com/sports/%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%95%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9B%E0%A7%87-%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%87-%E0%A6%86%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%9C%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%95-%E0%A6%AE%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9A%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%B9%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AC-|url-status=live}}</ref>

|2023–present

|style="background:#dfd;"|'''Win'''
|-

|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|India}}

|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|Nepal}}

|-

|-
|[[2003 SAFF Gold Cup#Final|Final]]
|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|Maldives}}

|style="background:#ffd;"|0–0

|style="background:#fdd;"|'''Loss'''

|style="background:#dfd;"|5–0
|style="background:#dfd;"|'''Win'''

===AFC Challenge Cup===
{| class="wikitable"  style="text-align: center;font-size:105%;width: 800px"
|-
!colspan=9 style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color: #FFFFFF;|[[AFC Challenge Cup|{{color|#FFFFFF|AFC Challenge Cup]]}} record||colspan=7 style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color: #FFFFFF;|{{color|#FFFFFF|Qualification}} record

!Position
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
!Squad

!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
| style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Bangladesh}} [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]] || Quarter-finals ||4 ||2 ||1 ||1 ||7 ||8 || [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup squads#Bangladesh|Squad]] ||colspan=7| ''Qualified as hosts''
|-
||{{flagicon|India}} [[2008 AFC Challenge Cup|2008]]||colspan=8| ''Did not qualify''||2 ||0 ||1 ||1||1 ||2
|-
||{{flagicon|Sri Lanka}} [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup|2010]]|| Group stage ||3 ||1 ||0 ||2||3 ||6|| [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup squads#Bangladesh|Squad]] ||3 ||2 ||0 ||1||5 ||2
|-
||{{flagicon|Nepal}} [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup|2012]]|| rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''||3 ||1 ||0 ||2||2 ||5
|-
||{{flagicon|Maldives}} [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup|2014]]||3 ||2 ||0 ||1||6 ||1
|-

===Asian Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:100%;width: 800px"
|-
!colspan=10 style="background-color:#006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;"|[[Football at the Asian Games|{{color|#FFFFFF|Asian Games record}}]]
|-

!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Matches won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Matches drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Matches lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! Squad
|-
|{{flagicon|IND}} [[Football at the 1951 Asian Games|1951 New Delhi]]
| rowspan="6" colspan="9" |''Did not enter; was part of {{fb|PAK}} until 1971''
|-

|-

|-

|-

|-

|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|Iran|1964}} [[Football at the 1974 Asian Games|1974 Tehran]] ||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|THA}} [[Football at the 1978 Asian Games|1978 Bangkok]]||Group stage||12/14||2||0||0||2||0||4||[[Football at the 1978 Asian Games – Men's team squads|Squad]]
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|IND}} [[Football at the 1982 Asian Games|1982 New Delhi]]||Group stage||11/16||3||1||0||2||2||4||[[Football at the 1982 Asian Games – Men's team squads#Bangladesh|Squad]]
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|KOR}} [[Football at the 1986 Asian Games|1986 Seoul]]||Group stage||16/18||4||1||0||3||1||12||[[Football at the 1986 Asian Games – Men's team squads#Bangladesh|Squad]]
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|CHN}} [[Football at the 1990 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1990 Beijing]]||Group stage||11/14||2||0||0||2||0||7||[[Football at the 1990 Asian Games – Men's team squads#Bangladesh|Squad]]
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|JPN}} [[Football at the 1994 Asian Games|1994 Hiroshima]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|- style="background:White;"
||{{flagicon|THA}} [[Football at the 1998 Asian Games|1998 Bangkok]]||colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR}} [[Football at the 2002 Asian Games|2002]] to present
| colspan="9" |''See [[Bangladesh national under-23 football team|Bangladesh U-23 national team]]''
|-
|style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color:white; "|'''Total'''
|style="background: #006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;color:white; "|'''4/13'''

|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|Japan}}
|style="background:#fdd;"|0–4
|style="background:#fdd;"|'''Loss'''
|-
|rowspan=2 |[[Football at the 1990 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1990]]
|rowspan=2 |[[Football at the 1990 Asian Games – Men's tournament#Group D|Group stage]]
|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|Saudi Arabia}}
|style="background:#fdd;"|0–4
|style="background:#fdd;"|'''Loss'''
|rowspan=2 |[[Xiannongtan Stadium]], {{flagicon|China}} China
|-
|style="text-align:left"|{{fb|Japan}}
|style="background:#fdd;"|0–3
|style="background:#fdd;"|'''Loss'''
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{Main|Bangladesh national football team results}}

{{As of|2026|6|5}}.<ref name="Record overall">{{cite web |url=https://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/statisticsandrecords/headtohead/index.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070613025803/http://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/statisticsandrecords/headtohead/index.html |url-status=dead |archive-date=13 June 2007 |title=Head to Head |publisher=FIFA |access-date=10 October 2016}}</ref><ref name="Record">{{cite web |url=http://www.eloratings.net/Bangladesh.htm |title=Bangladesh |website=World Football Elo Ratings |access-date=15 October 2016 |archive-date=11 October 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161011053217/http://www.eloratings.net/Bangladesh.htm |url-status=live }}</ref>

e.g. –&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;–&nbsp;– -->

{{legend2|FFFFCC|Wins equal losses|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|FFDACC|More losses|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

|-

!colspan=12 style="background-color:#006a4e;border: 2px solid #ff3300;"|<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">Last match updated was against [[San Marino national football team|{{color|Red|San Marino}}]]</span> <span style="color:#FFFFFF;">on 6 June 2026.</span>
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[SAFF Championship]]'''<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffcup.html|title=South Asian Football Federation Cup|publisher= [[RSSSF]]|access-date=5 June 2026|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605145314/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffcup.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Champions (1)''': [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup|2003]]
** {{silver02}} Runners-up (2): [[1999 SAFF Gold Cup|1999]], [[2005 SAFF Gold Cup|2005]]
** {{bronze03}} Third place (2): [[1995 SAARC Gold Cup|1995]]
* '''[[South Asian Games]]'''<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/safg.html|title=South Asian Federation Games|publisher= [[RSSSF]]|access-date=5 June 2026|archive-date=5 June 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260605145441/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/safg.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Gold medal (1)''': [[Football at the 1999 South Asian Games|1999]]
** {{silver02}} Silver medal (4): [[Football at the 1984 South Asian Games|1984]], [[Football at the 1985 South Asian Games|1985]], [[Football at the 1989 South Asian Games|1989]], [[Football at the 1995 South Asian Games|1995]]
** {{bronze03}} Bronze medal (1): [[Football at the 1991 South Asian Games|1991]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[Bangabandhu Cup|Bangabandhu Gold Cup]]'''
** {{silver02}} Runners-up (1): [[2015 Bangabandhu Cup|2015]]
* '''[[Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament]]'''<ref name="Quaid-E-Azam">{{cite web |url= https://www.rsssf.org/tablesq/quaid-e-azam.html |title= Quaid-E-Azam International Cup (Pakistan) |publisher= [[RSSSF]] |access-date= 8 September 2015 |archive-date= 13 July 2022 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220713143821/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesq/quaid-e-azam.html|url-status= live}}</ref>
** {{silver02}} Runners-up (1): 1985
** {{bronze03}} Third place (1): 1987
* '''[[Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|President's Gold Cup]]'''<ref name="President's Gold Cup">{{cite web |url= https://www.rsssf.org/tablesp/presgold.html |title= President's Gold Cup 1989 |publisher= [[RSSSF]] |access-date= 8 September 2015 |archive-date= 29 July 2022 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220729070858/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesp/presgold.html |url-status= live }}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Champions (1)''': [[1989 Bangladesh President's Gold Cup|1989]]
* '''[[Burma Tournament 1995|4-nation Tiger Trophy]]'''<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.utpalshuvro.com/special-article/news/1179|script-title=bn:'আন্তর্জাতিক' ফুটবলে প্রথম শিরোপা|website=Utp al Shuvro|language=bn|access-date=19 February 2022|archive-date=19 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220219054858/https://www.utpalshuvro.com/special-article/news/1179|url-status=live}}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Champions (1)''': [[Burma Tournament 1995|1995]]
* '''Jigme Dorji Wangchuk Memorial Trophy'''<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.thedailystar.net/news/bd-lift-jigme-dorji-trophy|title=BD lift Jigme Dorji trophy |work=The Daily Star|date=3 January 2003|archive-date=9 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231009165857/https://www.thedailystar.net/news/bd-lift-jigme-dorji-trophy|url-status=live}}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Champions (1)''': 2003
* '''Mujib Borsho FIFA International Football Series'''<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.unb.com.bd/category/Sports/fifa-intl-series-bangladesh-win-two-match-series/60652|title=FIFA Int'l Series: Bangladesh win two-match series|work=UNB|access-date=3 February 2022|archive-date=14 December 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211214065942/https://www.unb.com.bd/category/Sports/fifa-intl-series-bangladesh-win-two-match-series/60652|url-status=live}}</ref>
** {{gold01}} '''Champions (1)''': 2020
* '''[[2021 Three Nations Cup (Nepal)|Three Nations Cup (Nepal)]]'''
** {{silver02}} Runners-up (1): [[2021 Three Nations Cup (Nepal)|2021]]

==Further reading==
* {{Cite news |url=https://khulnagazette.com/%e0%a6%ab%e0%a7%81%e0%a6%9f%e0%a6%ac%e0%a6%b2%e0%a6%be%e0%a6%b0-%e0%a6%86%e0%a6%b8%e0%a6%b2%e0%a6%be%e0%a6%ae%e0%a7%87%e0%a6%b0-%e0%a6%86%e0%a6%b8%e0%a6%b2%e0%a6%be%e0%a6%ae-%e0%a6%b9%e0%a7%9f/ |script-title=bn:ফুটবলার আসলামের আসলাম হয়ে ওঠার গল্প |trans-title=The story of footballer Aslam's journey of becoming Aslam |date=20 September 2020 |access-date=5 February 2023 |archive-date=28 August 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220828133922/https://khulnagazette.com/%E0%A6%AB%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%9F%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%86%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AE%E0%A7%87%E0%A6%B0-%E0%A6%86%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%B2%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AE-%E0%A6%B9%E0%A7%9F/ |newspaper=খুলনা গেজেট &#124; সবার আগে সঠিক খবর |publisher=খুলনা গেজেট |language=bn |location=Khulna, Bangladesh |first=Dulal |last=Mahmud}}
